era: 1988-1999. Grunge. E=8. Georgetown, Seattle. Sub Pop Records. The Grunge Summit opens at degree 309.

location.origin: Georgetown, Seattle, Washington (Tad Doyle originally from Boise, Idaho)

key_albums: God's Balls (1989, Sub Pop) -- debut, Jack Endino, heaviest Sub Pop record at time
Salt Lick EP (1989, Sub Pop) -- Singles Club, European tour companion
8-Way Santa (1991, Sub Pop) -- definitive TAD record, Jinx, Giant Killer
Inhaler (1993, Sub Pop/East West) -- most produced, Throat Locust, Paladine
Infrared Riding Hood (1995, East West) -- final, J Mascis + Kim Thayil produced

theory_nodes.concept: THE GRUNGE SUMMIT OPENS. Degrees 309-316: the emotional peak of the entire 360-degree engine. TAD opens at E=8, the bridge between sludge metal (Melvins, 308) and grunge proper. The Summit escalates: TAD (E=8) + Mudhoney (E=8) + Nirvana (E=9) + Soundgarden (E=9).
theory_nodes.description: Grunge Summit opening: Georgetown, E=8, Sub Pop's heaviest act.

theory_nodes.concept: GEORGETOWN ENTERS. First Georgetown entry in the engine. Industrial southern Seattle: Duwamish River, Boeing Field, working-class geography. The Summit opens from the margins, not from Capitol Hill. Georgetown's industrial character frames the Summit's opening.
theory_nodes.description: Georgetown as Grunge Summit's geographic anchor: industrial periphery.

theory_nodes.concept: Tad Doyle: former professional butcher, competitive weightlifter, 300+ pounds. The physical biography directly produces the sonic character. Below-the-floor vocal register, low-end guitar density, sustained force over speed. The body is the instrument.
theory_nodes.description: Physical biography as sonic determinant in TAD's case.

theory_nodes.concept: 1989 European tour with Nirvana: the last moment before commercial divergence. Both Sub Pop, both unknown, equally received by European underground. Two years later Nevermind separates them permanently. The tour is the last moment they are peers.
theory_nodes.description: TAD-Nirvana European tour as pre-divergence peer moment.

theory_nodes.concept: Jack Endino produced both God's Balls (TAD) and Bleach (Nirvana) in the same period at Reciprocal Recording. The sonic continuity between the two most important Sub Pop acts at their foundational documents: same engineer, same facility, same philosophy.
theory_nodes.description: Endino as the connective tissue between TAD and Nirvana's foundational records.

cross_references.relationship: Sludge metal predecessor at E=8. TAD is the grunge bridge from Melvins sludge to accessible grunge: heavier than Mudhoney, more song-structured than Melvins. E=8 consecutive.
Sub Pop peer. E=8 Summit companion. Toured together. Different weight register: Mudhoney fuzz-and-attitude vs. TAD low-end density.
1989 European tour co-headliners. Pre-Nevermind peers. Both Sub Pop. Commercial divergence after Nevermind: Nirvana broke through; TAD did not.

sps_pair.species: Great Horned Owl pair at peak
sps_pair.pairing_note: E=8 + E=9 = E=17. Two owls simultaneous full-volume winter territorial claim paired with TAD's two-guitar heavy assault. Both at maximum winter intensity. Georgetown's industrial south and the owl territory's winter darkness.
